questions.question: What is the primary goal of clustering algorithms?
options: A) To predict the target variable
B) To group similar data points together
C) To identify outliers
D) To visualize data
questions.explanation: Clustering algorithms aim to group similar data points together based on their intrinsic structure.

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of clustering algorithm?
options: A) Linear Regression
B) K-Means
C) Decision Trees
D) Support Vector Machines
questions.explanation: K-Means is a type of unsupervised clustering algorithm that partitions the data into K clusters based on their similarities.

questions.question: What is the distance metric used in K-Means clustering?
options: A) Euclidean Distance
B) Manhattan Distance
C) Minkowski Distance
D) Cosine Distance
questions.explanation: Euclidean Distance is commonly used in K-Means clustering, as it measures the straight-line distance between two points.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'seed' value in K-Means clustering?
options: A) To specify the number of clusters
B) To initialize the centroid randomly
C) To determine the stopping criterion
D) To evaluate the clustering quality
questions.explanation: The 'seed' value is used to initialize the centroid of the clusters randomly, which helps in avoiding the same cluster structure.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of hierarchical clustering?
options: A) It partitions the data into K clusters
B) It groups similar data points together
C) It builds a tree-like structure of clusters
D) It uses a single distance metric
questions.explanation: Hierarchical clustering builds a tree-like structure of clusters, where each cluster is further divided until a stopping criterion is reached.
